name: Apple Plate Cake (Scottish)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F (230°C) or gas mark 7.
2. Grease an 8-inch round flan tin with the melted butter.
3. In a large bowl, rub the melted butter into the self-raising flour and stir in the caster sugar until well combined.
4. Peel and grate two of the apples and add to the flour mixture with the beaten egg. Mix well.
5. If the dough is firm, gradually add milk until it reaches a soft consistency.
6. Turn the dough onto a floured surface and knead lightly.
7. Press the dough evenly into the prepared tin.
8. Peel, core, and slice the remaining apples. Arrange them neatly on top of the dough in overlapping layers.
9. Brush the tops with melted butter and sprinkle with ground cloves or cinnamon and a little cannon sugar.
10. Bake for 25-30 minutes until golden brown and serve warm.
